2606.13349.title: From Passive Generation to Investigation: A Proactive Scientific Peer Review Agent
2606.13349.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have shown promise in automating scientific peer review. However, existing approaches often struggle to generate in-depth reviews supported by concrete evidence. We argue that a key limitation is the lack of flexibility to proactively investigate suspicious parts of a paper based on accumulated evidence, as human reviewers do. In this paper, we explore how to enable an LLM-based review agent to perform such proactive investigation. We find that this can be naturally formulated as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), and propose ProReviewer, a scientific peer review agent that proactively reviews a paper guided by a maintained, structured review log. The structured review log serves as a workspace for the agent to track evidence and intermediate findings collected during review. Experiments show that ProReviewer with an 8B backbone, trained by supervised fine-tuning and optimized by reinforcement learning, achieves the highest average score across five quality dimensions, outperforming prompt-based methods with much larger frontier LLMs by up to 39% and the strongest fine-tuned baseline by 16% relatively. It also attains the highest win rates against baselines in human evaluation.
2606.13349.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在自動化科學同行評審方面顯示出潛力。<lb/>然而，現有的方法往往難以生成有具體證據支持的深入評審。<lb/>我們認為，一個主要的限制是缺乏靈活性，無法根據累積的證據主動調查論文中可疑的部分，正如人類評審者所做的那樣。<lb/>在本文中，我們探討如何使基於LLM的評審代理能夠進行這種主動調查。<lb/>我們發現，這可以自然地表述為馬爾可夫決策過程（MDP），並提出ProReviewer，一個主動評審論文的科學同行評審代理，該代理由維護的結構化評審日誌指導。<lb/>結構化評審日誌作為代理的工作空間，用於跟踪在評審過程中收集的證據和中間發現。<lb/>實驗顯示，ProReviewer使用8B的基礎模型，通過監督微調訓練並通過強化學習優化，實現了五個質量維度中最高的平均分數，超越了基於提示的方法，這些方法使用了更大型的前沿LLMs，提升幅度高達39%，並且相對於最強的微調基準提升了16%。<lb/>它在人工評估中也達到了對基準的最高勝率。
2606.13349.author: Haishuo Fang et.al.
2606.13349.authors: Haishuo Fang, Yue Feng, Iryna Gurevych
